tweets.text:
We noted this last year (and it worked), but when the S&P 500 is up between 5-10% YTD at the midpoint of the year, the rest of the year usually does quite well.

In fact, worst full year return was exactly flat in 2011, so a large decline from here would be quite rare. 

So not too strong, but by no means weak. Yet another reason to continue riding the wave in 2026. 🌊🌊

tweets.text:
Why are central banks buying so much gold?

A record 90% of central banks cited gold's performance during times of crisis as a key factor in their decision to hold gold, according to the World Gold Council survey of 69 central banks.

This trend is more frequently seen in emerging market and developing economy central banks, with 92% citing crisis performance as the primary driver.

By comparison, this figure stands at 81% among advanced economy central banks.

Furthermore, gold's role as a long-term store of value and inflation hedge was cited by 84% of respondents, while 83% pointed to its effectiveness as a portfolio diversifier.

Meanwhile, 85% of emerging market central banks cited gold as a geopolitical hedge, compared to just 56% in advanced economies.

Gold remains at the core of central bank financial reserves.

tweets.text:
BREAKING: US M2 money supply surged +$247.8 billion in May, to a record $23.1 trillion.

This marks the largest monthly increase since May 2021.

Year-to-date, M2 has soared +$698.6 billion, the largest January to May increase in 5 years.

Money supply now stands $1.3 trillion above the March 2022 peak.

Since 2000, money in circulation has grown at an average annual rate of +6.3%.

US money creation is accelerating.

tweets.text:
BREAKING: Global gold-backed ETFs posted -38.3 tonnes in outflows last week, the largest weekly outflow since September 2022.

This was led by North America, at -23.6 tonnes, followed by Asia, at -8.7 tonnes, and Europe, at -5.9 tonnes.

In Dollar terms, these funds posted -$4.7 billion in withdrawals, the largest weekly outflow on record.

The largest US gold-backed ETF, $GLD, alone saw -$2.0 billion in outflows, the 4th-largest this year.

So far in June, $GLD has recorded -$3.2 billion in withdrawals, on track for its 2nd-worst month since February 2021, after a record -$8.5 billion in March.

Gold market sentiment remains highly volatile.

tweets.text:
🇺🇸 The dollar is retreating in global foreign-exchange reserves but not collapsing. More importantly, no other currency is really taking its place.

📉 Since the early 2000s, the dollar’s share of global foreign-exchange reserves has fallen from around 70% to roughly 55% today. A meaningful and structural decline that reflects a willingness from central banks to reduce their dependence on a single currency. Despite that decline, the dollar remains by far the world’s dominant reserve currency even if we see a slow erosion of its monopoly.

➡️ This decline is not really benefiting one major rival currency. The euro remains broadly stuck around 20%, sterling and the yen remain secondary, and the Chinese yuan has not taken off. Being a major economic power is not enough to turn a currency into a global reserve currency. It needs deep, open and liquid financial markets, free capital flows, strong legal credibility, and a risk-free asset large enough to absorb hundreds of billions of dollars. Today, the United States still has that advantage through the Treasury market, something China does not yet offer.

✅ Therefore, the real winner remains diversification as central banks are spreading their reserves across a broader set of currencies and also increasingly into gold.

In a more fragmented geopolitical world, holding assets that are not directly dependent on the US financial system becomes more attractive.

*Link: https://t.co/XZQm890PTj

🇪🇺 My proxies and regional surveys suggest #Eurozone CPI (June) will surprise downward.

*Bloomberg consensus:
+3.0%e v +3.2% prior

The slowdown could be explained by 3 main factors:

1️⃣ The decline of energy prices (gasoline prices)

2️⃣ Calendar effect normalization (post Easter holidays)

3️⃣ #France: Higher number of promotional sales days in the June collection period compared to last year.

tweets.text:
🇨🇳 #China | AI Demand, Price Rebound Drive A Shares’ 1H Earnings - Shanghai Securities News

➡ Most of the 35 mainland China-listed firms that have released 2026 first-half earnings previews posted upbeat results, with AI, lithium and petrochemical sectors leading growth, the Shanghai Securities News reports.

➡ Global AI boom fuels electronics profit expansion, with chip and equipment leaders including Hangzhou Chang Chuan Technology Co. and Shenzhen Fine Made Electronics Group Co. reporting strong resultsConsumer electronics and display panel players, however, remain under pressure due to weak demand and intensifying competition.

➡ Demand for power and energy storage batteries drives volumes and prices for raw material suppliers, with EVE Energy and Yongtai Technology among outstanding performers.

➡ Price recovery and integrated operational advantages drive earnings surge at petrochemical leaders Hengyi Petrochemical and Satellite Chemical, raising optimism the industry is exiting from a cyclical downturn.

*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/HaHkDmycCH

thread_tweets.text:
For years, Apple was the company with all the pricing power.

Now the AI boom is showing that even the strongest consumer brands can become price takers when a critical input is in short supply.

But this imbalance won't last forever - the cure for high prices is high prices.
